meaning: adj.未磨光的,无光泽的,未磨练的,粗鲁的
examples: Afternoon lowers on evening; the sky is the color of 5 unpolished silver.
Its green surroundings showcase a ruby from Tanzania in its unpolished beauty.
"The light is very bright and pleasant but the bar is unpolished," the waiter said.
But Virgin said the pitch--which included Merkley staffers' written ideas taped to windows--seemed unpolished.
Walk this route and you will discover a place where mountain life has retained an authentic, unpolished edge.
